campaign.name: AAA AI Circle Activation Map
campaign.objective: Review a target-backed AI KOL execution pool and turn client feedback into reusable selection rules.

ui.sideNote: 客户版只展示可执行路径；每一次选择都会写入后端 event log。

hero.title: 用“被看到”和“被互动”两条线反推可执行 KOL。
hero.lede: iLands 的目标不是先找泛 AI KOL，而是把客户反馈前置成可追踪的 selection board：客户在前端 approve、reject、question、hold，agency 在后端拿到完整决策记录、否决原因和 follow-up。

pool.title: 候选 KOL 直接进入客户可见执行池，再用反馈回流排序。
pool.description: 这里不是 discovery。候选已经被 agency 放入 campaign pool；客户只负责审核、否决、提问和确认。页面刷新后状态来自后端 current state，历史来自 append-only event log。

learning.title: 先把“AI 行业大佬”翻译成可被客户修正的判别逻辑。
learning.description: 每一轮 KOL 反馈都会回到这里：Approve 强化相似人群，Reject 形成扣分规则，Question 保留成下一轮需要验证的边界案例。
learning.systemDescription: 初始需求：客户要找“AI 行业大佬”。当前版本先假设客户真正要的是能影响 AI 圈讨论、能带来投资/BD 连接，或能让产品叙事更可信的人。

sections.title: 更想要的人
sections.subtitle: Approve 会强化这些信号
defaultRules: 技术圈或产品圈有可信权威，能让讨论不只是硬广。
能带来 warm intro、投资兴趣、BD 连接或高质量二跳可见面。
和 AI agent、character / social AI、AI-native app layer 有真实关系。
优先真实个人账号，而不是公司官方号或泛媒体账号。

sections.title: 不想要的人
sections.subtitle: Reject 会形成扣分规则
defaultRules: 泛 AI 工具号、prompt hack 账号、纯流量型 newsletter。
官方公司号、机构号，除非只作为远端 root 而不是可投放 KOL。
和 iLands 叙事太远，无法自然解释为什么会看到这个产品。
品牌、关系或竞品风险太高，容易让合作显得不自然。

sections.title: 还在确认的边界
sections.subtitle: Question 会进入下一轮验证题
defaultRules: 纯学术人物是可信背书目标，还是离商业转化太远。
超大号更适合当远端 root，还是值得尝试直接触达。
VC root 更看重投资 fit、warm intro，还是公开信息流可见性。
客户是否接受媒体 / podcast 作为二跳路径，而不是直接 KOL。

sections.title: 下一轮推荐如何调整
sections.subtitle: 反馈会进入 root / KOL 排序
defaultRules: Approve 的 KOL 会扩散更多相似身份、组织、话题和二跳关注对象。
Reject 的原因会变成扣分项，例如媒体化、离叙事太远、品牌敏感。
Question 的对象保留为边界样本，下一轮继续问客户“到底要不要”。
下一轮名单保持 70% 高匹配、20% 边界确认、10% 探索样本。

rules.title: KOL List 判别逻辑
rules.description: 下面负责解释为什么一个账号会进入执行池、为什么排到 A/B/C/D，以及客户对单个 KOL 的 Approve / Reject / Question 会怎样回流到下一轮排序。

sections.title: 为什么进入 KOL List
sections.subtitle: 进入执行池的最低条件
rules: 能把内容送进某类 root 平时会看的信息流。
内容方向和 iLands 的 AI agent / AI community / AI-native social 叙事有关。
账号必须有真实表达面：近期内容、受众、互动或媒体路径能被检查。

sections.title: 优先推进谁
sections.subtitle: Approve 会强化这些信号
rules: A 类优先确认报价、样张、投放档期和可定制角度。
B 类先做 BD 验证：联系方式、接单意愿、历史合作和报价区间。
C 类走 warm intro、PR 或媒体库存，不按普通 paid KOL 处理。

sections.title: 需要补充验证
sections.subtitle: Question 会进入核查清单
rules: 需要补近期样张、真实曝光、评论区回复者质量或 sponsor 入口。
需要解释这个 KOL 对应哪类 root，以及为什么该 root 可能会看到。
需要确认这是可合作账号、媒体路径，还是只适合内部观察的参考账号。

sections.title: 什么会扣分
sections.subtitle: Reject 会形成排除或降权规则
rules: 泛工具号、prompt hack 账号或纯流量内容会降权。
和目标 root 的信息流关系弱、内容角度牵强、看到后价值不明确会降权。
品牌、竞品、身份或合作风险太高时，不进入本轮优先推进。

candidates.name: Lena Vos
candidates.bio: Writes practical analysis on applied AI systems, inference economics, and founder-led launches.
candidates.audienceSummary: AI founders, infrastructure operators, seed-to-Series B investors, and technical PMs.
candidates.whyIncluded: Strong overlap with budget-owning AI operators and a track record of sober product explainers.
candidates.recommendedAngle: Use a concise launch thread around the operating bottleneck the product removes.
candidates.clientFacingNote: Best fit for a credibility-first launch, not a broad awareness push.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Warm intro path through prior newsletter sponsor. Ask for thread + quote bundle.

initial.note: Strong audience and credible technical tone.

candidates.name: Omar Klein
candidates.bio: Long-form technical educator covering AI developer tooling and production engineering.
candidates.audienceSummary: Senior developers, solution architects, and AI platform teams evaluating tools.
candidates.whyIncluded: High-trust demo format and strong watch time on implementation-heavy content.
candidates.recommendedAngle: Sponsor a build-through walkthrough with one concrete workflow and a clear before/after.
candidates.clientFacingNote: Good for explaining complexity, but turnaround is slower than social-first channels.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Requires a detailed brief and demo environment two weeks before filming.

initial.note: Please confirm current pricing and last 90 day view range.

candidates.name: Mira Chen
candidates.bio: Weekly essays on AI go-to-market, enterprise adoption, and founder positioning.
candidates.audienceSummary: Founders, GTM leads, product marketers, and early enterprise buyers.
candidates.whyIncluded: Editorial tone matches the client-facing narrative and can frame the category cleanly.
candidates.recommendedAngle: Place a sponsored note around the buyer decision and link to a launch memo.
candidates.clientFacingNote: A premium narrative channel for decision-makers rather than pure traffic.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Newsletter sponsor calendar has limited July slots.

candidates.name: Nadia Hart
candidates.bio: Visual case studies on product workflows, design systems, and AI-assisted creative operations.
candidates.audienceSummary: Design leads, creative operators, and product teams inside growth-stage software companies.
candidates.whyIncluded: Can translate technical value into crisp workflow visuals for a non-engineering buyer.
candidates.recommendedAngle: Carousel showing the old review loop versus the new decision workflow.
candidates.clientFacingNote: Useful if the launch needs a polished creative-ops angle.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Brand safety is clean; past sponsored content is polished but less technical.

initial.note: Maybe useful for creative angle after the technical launch list is locked.

candidates.name: Theo Park
candidates.bio: Interview host focused on infrastructure founders, product strategy, and technical GTM.
candidates.audienceSummary: Founders, operators, and investor listeners who prefer deeper context over short posts.
candidates.whyIncluded: Strong host credibility and a format suited for explaining an unfamiliar category.
candidates.recommendedAngle: Founder interview with concrete customer problem, not a generic launch spot.
candidates.clientFacingNote: Good for authority building, weaker for immediate traffic.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Ask for episode clip rights for social reuse.

candidates.name: Jules Gray
candidates.bio: Short-form creator covering startup tactics, growth myths, and founder productivity.
candidates.audienceSummary: Broad startup audience with heavy junior founder and creator skew.
candidates.whyIncluded: Large reach and fast turnaround, but fit depends on how broad the campaign should go.
candidates.recommendedAngle: A myth-busting short on how AI products should measure traction.
candidates.clientFacingNote: High reach, lower precision. Worth using only if awareness is the priority.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Audience quality varies. Request story view and saves before recommending strongly.

initial.note: Reach is attractive, but the audience is too broad for this round.

candidates.name: Iris O'Kane
candidates.bio: Research translator explaining model evaluation, synthetic data, and benchmark reliability.
candidates.audienceSummary: ML researchers, applied AI engineers, and technical founders.
candidates.whyIncluded: Gives the campaign research credibility without turning it into an academic pitch.
candidates.recommendedAngle: Quote post or short thread on why evaluation quality matters before scaling spend.
candidates.clientFacingNote: Best as a credibility layer alongside a larger launch voice.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Avoid over-commercial copy. Works better with technical proof points.

candidates.name: Alex Ramos
candidates.bio: Podcast and newsletter covering software markets, startup finance, and investor narratives.
candidates.audienceSummary: Investors, CFO-minded founders, and GTM leaders tracking category momentum.
candidates.whyIncluded: Useful if the campaign needs investor and market narrative credibility.
candidates.recommendedAngle: Discuss the budget decision pressure behind the category, not only product features.
candidates.clientFacingNote: Good for investor-facing credibility and market framing.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Potential conflict with a competitor sponsor. Check exclusivity before outreach.

candidates.name: Sasha Wu
candidates.bio: Hands-on AI tooling reviewer with clear benchmarks, demos, and buyer checklists.
candidates.audienceSummary: AI builders, developer advocates, and hands-on operators comparing tool stacks.
candidates.whyIncluded: Can make the product tangible through a controlled demo and measured comparison.
candidates.recommendedAngle: Review workflow using a real campaign scenario and before/after benchmarks.
candidates.clientFacingNote: Strong if we can provide a clean demo path and measured proof.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Needs product access and sandbox data before committing.

candidates.name: Hana Lee
candidates.bio: Operator newsletter on lean GTM systems, sales process design, and marketing execution discipline.
candidates.audienceSummary: Operators, GTM managers, agency founders, and B2B service leaders.
candidates.whyIncluded: Good fit for decision-process messaging and lower-friction sponsored notes.
candidates.recommendedAngle: Frame the console as a way to remove scattered client feedback from KOL execution.
candidates.clientFacingNote: More agency/operator fit than pure AI buyer fit.
candidates.agencyInternalNote: Affordable test slot; consider as backup if budget tightens.
